subject: [PATCH v4 4/9] mm/memory_hotplug: add __add_memory_driver_managed() with online_type arg

2026-06-05.patch_summary: This patch adds a new interface to the Linux kernel's memory hotplug functionality, allowing drivers to select their preferred online type (ZONE_NORMAL or ZONE_MOVABLE) when adding memory. The CXL driver is the primary motivator for this change, as it needs to plumb a preferred policy through the dax kmem driver. The patch refactors existing APIs to add a new helper function that accepts an explicit online type and validates its value. This change does not introduce any functional changes for existing users but provides a more flexible way for drivers to manage memory hotplug.

reviews.author: David (Arm)
reviews.summary: Reviewer David (Arm) pointed out inconsistent indentation in the patch, specifically requesting two-tab indent on second parameter lines for new code and existing instances.
reviews.raw_body:
We prefer two-tab indent on second parameter line while touching code / adding
new code.

Same applies to the other instances below.


Apart from that (still) LGTM.

-- 
Cheers,

David

2026-06-09.patch_summary: This patch adds a new function, __add_memory_driver_managed(), which allows drivers to select the preferred online type (ZONE_NORMAL or ZONE_MOVABLE) when adding memory. The existing add_memory_driver_managed() function is refactored to use this new helper, and proper kernel documentation is added. This change is primarily for the CXL driver, but it's designed to be safe for in-tree modules that need to override the system-wide onlining policy.
